finalizeLoan(address,bytes,bytes).details: Reverts with KMSInvalidSigner if the cleartext or proof is forged,         or if the proof belongs to a different ciphertext.

params.abiEncodedClearVerdict: ABI-encoded bool from the relayer's publicDecrypt.
params.borrower: The borrower whose request is being settled.
params.decryptionProof: KMS decryption proof from the relayer's publicDecrypt.

requestLoan(uint256).details: Requires the borrower to have called authorizeLender(this pool) on         Redact first, otherwise this contract has no ACL permission on the         verdict handle and the FHE ops revert.

params.amount: The loan amount requested (bounded by maxLoanPerUser).

InvalidKMSSignatures().notice: Returned if the returned KMS signatures are not valid.

ZamaProtocolUnsupported().notice: Returned if the Zama protocol is not supported on the current chain

PublicDecryptionVerified(bytes32[],bytes.notice: This event is emitted when public decryption has been successfully verified.

cancelRequest().notice: Cancel your own pending request (e.g. if you changed your mind         before finalization).

finalizeLoan(address,bytes,bytes).notice: Step 2 of the loan flow. Verifies the KMS decryption proof for the         borrower's verdict and settles the loan request accordingly.

repay(uint256).notice: Simple repay function to close out a loan in the demo.

requestLoan(uint256).notice: Step 1 of the loan flow. Marks the borrower's encrypted verdict as         publicly decryptable and records the pending request.

userdoc.notice: A demo undercollateralized lending pool that consumes verdicts from         the Redact confidential credit oracle, using the FHEVM v0.11         self-relaying decryption model.         Flow:           1. Borrower has already submitted an encrypted application to Redact.           2. Borrower calls redact.authorizeLender(address(this)) to consent.           3. Borrower calls requestLoan(amount). The pool marks the encrypted              verdict as publicly decryptable and records the pending request.           4. Offchain, the borrower's client calls publicDecrypt via the Zama              relayer SDK, receiving the clear verdict and a KMS decryption proof.           5. Anyone submits finalizeLoan(borrower, clearVerdict, proof).              The contract rebuilds the ciphertext handle list and calls              FHE.checkSignatures, which reverts unless the cleartext is the              cryptographically genuine decryption of the stored verdict.           6. If approved, the loan is credited. The raw features and the              numeric score never left encryption at any point. The only value              ever revealed is the final yes/no, and only because the borrower              chose to take a loan.
